description_long: This bird has a streamlined body with a rich chestnut-brown coloration covering its back, wings, and tail, while its head and upper chest are a deep slate-gray to black. The beak is short, straight, and pointed, blending seamlessly with the dark head. The wings are neatly folded along the sides, and the tail is long and tapers to a subtle point. The plumage appears smooth and well-groomed, with no visible patterning except for the clear color demarcation between the head and body. The legs are not visible in these views, but the overall form suggests a medium-sized passerine bird commonly found in urban parks and gardens.
description: A medium-sized bird with a chestnut-brown body, long pointed tail, and a dark gray-black head and beak. The plumage is smooth with a clear color contrast between the head and body.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's elongated body, chestnut-brown back and tail, and dark head with a pointed beak.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the layered brown tail feathers and the rounded back, with the head barely visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, displaying the same color contrast and body proportions.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the dark head, pointed beak, and the gradual transition to the brown body.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid brown body with a sharply contrasting dark head and upper chest
